nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

variants.name: Nevells
meanings: Irish: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevils
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Novel
meanings: English: variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newell
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

English: variant of   Newhall  .

variants.name: Nevills
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Newill
meanings: English and Irish (or Norman origin): variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Geoffrey
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Of Germanic (Frankish and Lombard) origin, introduced to Britain by the Normans. It was in regular use among the counts of Anjou, ancestors of the English royal house of Plantagenet, who were descended from Geoffrey Plantagenet, Count of Anjou ( 1113–51 ). It was a particularly popular name in England and France in the later Middle Ages; notable bearers in England include the poet Geoffrey Chaucer ( c.1340–1400 ) and in Wales the chronicler Geoffrey of Monmouth ( d. 1155 ). The original form and meaning of the name is disputed. According to one theory, it is merely a variant of Godfrey ; others derive the first part from the Germanic word gawia ‘territory’, walah ‘stranger’, or gisil ‘pledge’. Medieval forms can be found to support all these theories, and it is possible that several names have fallen together, or that the name was subjected to reanalysis by folk etymology at an early date.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When Geoffrey de Neville was born about 1080, in Walcot, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Gilbert de Neville, was 40 and his mother, Philicia de Damoys, was 21. He had at least 1 son and 1 daughter with Madame Johanna De Clere. He died in 1146, in Scothern, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 67, and was buried in Walcot near Billinghay, Lincolnshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Geoffrey de Neville est né environ 1080, à Walcot, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Gilbert de Neville, avait 40 ans et sa mère, Philicia de Damoys, avait 21 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille avec Madame Johanna De Clere. Il est décédé en 1146, à Scothern, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 67 ans, et a été enterré à Walcot near Billinghay, Lincolnshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als Geoffrey de Neville um 1080 in Walcot, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Gilbert de Neville 40 und seine Mutter Philicia de Damoys 21. Er hatte mit Madame Johanna De Clere mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Er starb 1146 im Alter von 67 Jahren in Scothern, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Walcot near Billinghay, Lincolnshire, England begraben.

Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia con Madame Johanna De Clere. Morì nel 1146, nel Scothern, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 67 anni, e fu sepolto a Walcot near Billinghay, Lincolnshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Nevilleが約1080年にイギリスリンコルンシュアイアワルコトで生まれた時，父Gilbert de Nevilleは40歳，母Philicia de Damoysは21歳であった。彼はMadame Johanna De Clereとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1146年にイギリスイギリスリンコルンシュアイアスコスエルンで67歳で死去し，イギリスリンコルンシュアイアワルコト ネアル ビリンガイにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Geoffrey de Neville(이)가 대략 1080년 영국 링코실르 월컷에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Gilbert de Neville(은)는 40세였고, 그의 어머니 Philicia de Damoys(은)는 21세였다. 그는 Madame Johanna De Clere(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1146년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 스컷헤른에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 링코실르 월컷 니아어 빌링하이에 묻혔다.

Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha com Madame Johanna De Clere. Ele faleceu em 1146, em Scothern, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 67 anos, e foi sepultado em Walcot near Billinghay, Lincolnshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Geoffrey de Neville родился около 1080 г. в местности валкот, линколншир, Англия, его отцу по имени Gilbert de Neville было 40 лет, а матери, которую звали Philicia de Damoys, 21. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь от Madame Johanna De Clere. Он умер в 1146 г. в местности скотирн, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 67 лет и был похоронен в местности валкот нир биллингхеи, линколншир, Англия.
